requirement: Fix finding F1 from .cdr/runs/2026-07-09/015-verification (verdict PASS_WITH_COMMENTS, medium severity): PAGE_MARKER_RE's non-greedy backreference-based parsing silently truncates a page's real content when that content contains a literal substring matching its own closing marker (e.g. literal '[[/PAGE 1]]' inside page 1's own extracted text). This directly violates subtask 3.3.1's literal acceptance criterion 'no page content dropped'. Marker-pair count parity is unaffected, so the existing count-based test does not catch it.
architecture_discovery: agents/ingestion/normalize_pdf.py is a standalone module with no other consumers yet (grep-confirmed: no other .py file in agents/ references PAGE_MARKER_RE, normalize_pdf, or the [[PAGE marker format). Free to change the wire format without breaking call sites; only agents/ingestion/test_normalize_pdf.py needs updating in lockstep.
impact_analysis: Blast radius limited to agents/ingestion/normalize_pdf.py and its test file. Future consumer (agents/ingestion/dispatch.py, not yet built per module docstring) will need to use the new iter_pages() API instead of a content-scanning regex -- documented in the module docstring to steer future implementers away from re-introducing the same bug class.
plan: 1. Reproduce F1 with a throwaway script before any fix, using page text containing a literal '[[/PAGE 1]]' substring, to confirm the exact silent-truncation failure mode.

2. Replace the [[PAGE n]]...[[/PAGE n]] content-scanning format with a length-prefixed [[PAGE n LEN=k]]...[[/PAGE n]] format: the header declares the exact character count of the payload, so a parser slices exactly k characters instead of scanning payload content for a closing marker -- eliminating the delimiter-in-payload collision class entirely (not just the reproduced case).
3. Add iter_pages() as the new public parsing API; keep PAGE_MARKER_RE (now header-only) for boundary-location use cases, but document it as unsafe for content extraction.

4. Update agents/ingestion/test_normalize_pdf.py: switch existing tests from PAGE_MARKER_RE.finditer content extraction to iter_pages(); add regression tests for F1 (self-referential marker-lookalike content, and a second test for a different page's marker-lookalike content) proving round-trip content integrity.
5. Add cheap F2 coverage: multi-digit page numbers (>=10), nonexistent-path negative test, corrupt-file negative test.
6. Run pytest 3x for flakiness; run ruff check on both changed files.
7. Commit locally (no push), update .cdr/index/regression.jsonl to mark F1 resolved, write this run's artifacts and handoff.json with confirmed final commit hash.

validation_matrix.criterion: No page content dropped, even when page text contains its own literal close-marker substring
No page content dropped when page text contains a different page's marker-lookalike substring
Existing behavior preserved: markers present in order, content preserved, marker count matches page count, empty pages still marked
validation_matrix.test: test_page_markers_present_in_order / test_page_content_preserved / test_marker_count_matches_page_count / test_empty_page_still_gets_marker (all updated to use iter_pages where content is asserted)

validation_matrix.criterion: F2: multi-digit page numbers parse correctly
F2: nonexistent/corrupt file negative cases raise
